title: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis
intro: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis, also known as Thai chestnut oak, is a species of tree in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to Thailand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as diabetes, hypertension, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus kemmaratensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tea and capsule extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to alleviate symptoms of certain diseases.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis is classified as a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus and is native to Southeast Asia. The species is further categorized into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Tracheophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Fagales. The classification reflects the tree's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the beech family. This classification helps botanists and researchers understand the tree's place within the plant kingdom.
uses: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis, a species of tree native to Thailand, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its leaves and bark have been traditionally used in Thai folk medicine to treat various ailments. The tree contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. However, further research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal benefits. Some studies suggest that Lithocarpus kemmaratensis may have antiviral and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising candidate for natural remedies.
benefits: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis, also known as the Thai chestnut oak, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Thailand due to its potential health benefits. Its leaves and bark contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Lithocarpus kemmaratensis may have anti-diabetic and anti-hypertensive effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential health benefits.

preparations: Lithocarpus kemmaratensis, also known as Kemmarat's Burma tit, is a species of evergreen tree native to Myanmar.
